title: Introduction to Nuxt.js
content:
### What is Nuxt.js?

Nuxt.js is a **meta-framework** built on top of Vue.js that provides powerful features like server-side rendering (SSR), static site generation (SSG), file-based routing, and auto-imports out of the box.

### SSR / SSG / SPA Modes

Nuxt 3 supports three rendering modes:

| Mode | Description | Use Case |
|---|---|---|
| **SSR** (Universal) | Renders on the server, hydrates on the client | SEO-critical, dynamic content |
| **SSG** (Static) | Pre-renders all pages at build time | Blogs, marketing sites |
| **SPA** | Client-side only, no server | Internal dashboards, apps |

### Nuxt 3 Key Features

- **File-based routing**: Pages automatically map to routes based on the `pages/` directory
- **Auto-imports**: Components, composables, and utilities are auto-imported — no manual imports needed
- **Server engine**: Nitro — a cross-platform server engine for Node.js, serverless, and edge
- **Hybrid rendering**: Mix SSR, SSG, and SPA per route in the same project
- **TypeScript**: First-class TypeScript support

### Creating a Nuxt 3 Project

```bash
npx nuxi@latest init my-nuxt-app
cd my-nuxt-app
npm run dev
```

### Project Structure

```
my-nuxt-app/
├── app.vue            # Root component
├── pages/             # File-based routing
├── components/        # Auto-imported components
├── composables/       # Auto-imported composables
├── layouts/           # Layout components
├── middleware/        # Route middleware
├── server/            # Server routes and API
├── nuxt.config.ts     # Nuxt configuration
└── package.json
```

quiz.question: What is Nuxt.js?
options: A state management library for Vue

quiz.question: Which Nuxt 3 feature auto-imports components and composables?
